verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, with some tasks becoming automated but human teachers still essential for complex decision-making and relationships.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 6, and 8 are largely automated by AI-powered educational tools, but human teachers still need to adapt these tools for individual students' needs.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment Needed
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 3, 10, and 12 require human teachers to make complex decisions about student behavior and academic progress, which are harder for AI systems to replicate.

evidence_ledger.contribution: O*NET task list provides authoritative data on tasks required for the occupation
Wikipedia summary highlights the importance of human teachers in complex decision-making and relationships

adjacent_careers.name: Instructional Designer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Instructional designers create educational materials, but human teachers are still needed to adapt these materials for individual students' needs.

adjacent_careers.name: Tutor
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Tutors work one-on-one with students, requiring human teachers to provide guidance and support

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered educational tool approved for use in elementary schools
recent_developments.impact: This new tool will automate some routine tasks, but human teachers are still essential for complex decision-making and relationships.

recent_developments.headline: Universities update curricula to assume AI in the loop
recent_developments.impact: Programs add prompt-craft, verification, and AI-direction skills as core competencies.

quotes.quote: We are at the cusp of using AI for probably the biggest positive transformation that education has ever seen — a personal tutor for every student, on every subject.
quotes.speaker: Sal Khan
quotes.title: Founder, Khan Academy

quotes.quote: If we just use AI to grade essays faster, we have failed. The opportunity is to free teachers from low-value work so they can do what only humans can do — mentor.
quotes.speaker: Linda Darling-Hammond
quotes.title: President, Learning Policy Institute; Stanford emerita

quotes.quote: Teachers are not going to be replaced by AI. Teachers who use AI well are going to replace teachers who do not.
quotes.speaker: Ethan Mollick
quotes.title: Associate Professor, Wharton; author of Co-Intelligence
